key_concepts: Biodiversity and Need for classification
Three domains of life, taxonomy and systematics
Concept of species and taxonomical hierarchy
Binomial nomenclature
Five kingdom classification system
Salient features of Monera, Protista and Fungi
Classification into major groups: Lichens, Viruses and Viroids
Classification of plants into major groups
Salient and distinguishing features: Algae, Bryophyta, Pteridophyta, Gymnospermae, Angiosperms
Salient features and classification of non-chordates up to phyla level
Classification of chordates upto class level

units.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

key_concepts: Morphology of root, stem, leaf, inflorescence, flower, fruit and seed
Anatomy and functions of tissue systems in dicots
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of digestive system (frog)
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of circulatory system (frog)
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of respiratory system (frog)
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of nervous system (frog)
Morphology, Anatomy and functions of reproductive system (frog)
Cell theory and cell as the basic unit of life
Structure of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells; Plant vs Animal cell
Structure and function of proteins, carbohydrates, lipids, nucleic acids
The cell cycle
Site of photosynthesis and pigments involved
Cellular respiration: glycolysis, fermentation (anaerobic), TCA cycle
Seed germination; phases of plant growth
Flower structure and gametophyte development
Pollination types, agencies, and out-breeding devices
Pollen-pistil interaction and double fertilization
Post-fertilization events: endosperm, embryo, seed, fruit formation
Special modes: apomixis, parthenocarpy, polyembryony
Male and female reproductive systems and microscopic anatomy
Gametogenesis: spermatogenesis and oogenesis
Menstrual cycle, fertilization, and blastocyst formation
Implantation, pregnancy, placenta formation (elementary)
Parturition and lactation (elementary)
Need for reproductive health and STD prevention
Birth control methods, contraception, and MTP
Amniocentesis procedures
Infertility and assisted reproductive technologies (IVF, ZIFT, GIFT)
Mendelian inheritance and deviations (incomplete dominance, co-dominance)
Multiple alleles, blood groups, pleiotropy, polygenic inheritance
Chromosome theory of inheritance and sex determination mechanisms
Linkage, crossing over, and sex-linked inheritance disorders
Mendelian and chromosomal disorders in humans
Search for genetic material and DNA structure (DNA/RNA)
DNA packaging, replication, and Central Dogma processes
Transcription, genetic code, translation, gene expression regulation
Lac operon model of gene regulation
Genome projects and DNA fingerprinting
Origin of life and evidences for evolution (paleontology, anatomy, embryology)
Darwin's contribution and modern synthetic theory
Mechanisms of evolution: variation via mutation and recombination
